Tournament Overview

The 31st Annual Rough Riders Charity Fishing Tournament benefits the 1st U.S. Volunteer Cavalry Regiment – Rough Riders, Inc. This is a boat tournament — catches by any fisherman on the boat are submitted as part of the boat. All winnings are paid to the boat. This is a skill-based competition; entry fees are for participation and administration only and do not constitute wagers or bets.

📅 Lines In: 6:00 AM  ·  Fishing Ends: 4:00 PM  ·  Offshore weigh-in must be acknowledged "in line" at Fusion Hotel by 4:00 PM

⚓ Boundaries: Gulf of America — New Pass to north tip of Anclote Island  ·  Inland — Oldsmar to New Pass. All fishing must comply with FWC and applicable regulations.

Entry Fees & Eligibility

FeeAmountDetails
Boat Registration$300Required per boat · up to 4 fishermen
Division Entry$100Per division · min 1 required · boats can compete in all 3
TWT Entry$100Optional · per division registered · must opt in before tournament start

To win in any division you must be pre-registered for that division. All entry fees must be paid prior to tournament start. All anglers must possess valid fishing licenses.

Divisions & Scoring

🐟 Inshore Division Photo · Length

  • Species: Redfish and Snook
  • Scoring: Total length of the two largest fish — one of each species
  • Catch, photograph, and release · submissions via official app within 1 hour
  • Playing card visible as identifier · fish on official measuring device · mouth closed · tail positioned
  • Measurements to nearest ¼ inch

🎣 Offshore Division Weigh-In

  • Species: Red Grouper and American Red Snapper
  • Entry Limit: Two (2) Grouper and two (2) Red Snapper per boat
  • Scoring: Aggregate weight of all four fish
  • Weigh-In: Fusion Hotel waterside — 290 107th Ave N, Treasure Island · Scales 1:00–4:00 PM Sat June 20
  • Fish must be fresh and unaltered; all foreign objects removed prior to weigh-in
  • Must be acknowledged in line by 4:00 PM — no late arrivals accepted

🦈 Tarpon Division Release · Count

  • Minimum size: 48 inches lip to fork (estimated) · micro tarpon not eligible
  • Scoring: Each valid leadered release counts as one (1)
  • Catch, photograph, and release only — no weigh-in permitted
  • Max 8 rods per boat · all anglers fish from same boat · land-based prohibited
  • Leader in hand + playing card visible in photo required
  • Up to 4 anglers may register releases per boat
  • Tie: Team to release the final scoring fish wins (based on photo receipt time)

Tournament Within a Tournament (TWT)

🏆 TWT — Optional Side Competition · $100 per division

  • Conducted concurrently with each fishing division (Inshore, Offshore, Tarpon)
  • All fish must be caught during official tournament hours
  • All applicable division rules, scoring methods, and regulations apply
  • Only boats that opt in prior to the start of the tournament are eligible for TWT prizes
  • Boats may enter TWT only for divisions in which they are officially registered
  • The highest-placing boat in each division that has entered the TWT will be awarded 90% of the total TWT entry pool for that division
  • This is a skill-based competition — TWT fees are not wagers or bets

    • Tackle, Photo & Release Rules

    Tackle Fishing Methods

    • Rod and reel only — manually operated · artificial lures and/or natural bait permitted
    • Leaders may not exceed 10 feet · no tackle modification during the fight
    • No electric reels, spearfishing, snagging, or gigging

    Photos Photo Submissions

    • All submissions via the official app within 1 hour — stored or external photos not accepted
    • Photos must be clear, unaltered, unobstructed · one fish per photo
    • Missing required elements (playing card, measuring board, leader in hand) will result in rejection

    ⚠️ Photos must be taken within the official app. Stored or external photos are not accepted.

    Tarpon Tarpon Handling

    • Fish must remain completely in the water at all times — no lifting
    • Fish must swim away under its own power · dead or improperly handled fish will not be scored
    • Excessive handling or intentional harm may result in immediate disqualification

    Awards, Protests & Waiver

    Prizes Awards & Tie Breakers

    • 1st Place: $5,000 per division  ·  2nd: $1,000  ·  3rd: $500
    • Tie Breakers: earliest valid submission (photo divisions) · earliest weigh-in (offshore)

    Protests Protest Procedure

    • Must be submitted in writing by 7:00 PM Saturday June 20, 2026 with $200 deposit
    • Decisions by the Rough Rider Fishing Tournament Committee are final and non-appealable
    • Prize payouts will not be delayed pending protests

    ⚠️ Disqualification grounds: Rule violations · Fraudulent submissions · Legal violations · Unsportsmanlike conduct. All decisions are final.

    Waiver Liability

    Participants fish at their own risk. The 1st U.S. Volunteer Cavalry Regiment – Rough Riders, Inc., and all affiliated parties assume no liability for injury, loss, or damage to the fullest extent permitted by Florida law.

    You're Registered!
    See you on the water — Bully!
